blockquote.p: -30- A type T is enumerable from a point P if either

li.p: (30.1) — T is a class type complete at point P or
(30.2) — T is an enumeration type defined by a declaration D such that D is reachable from P but P does not occur within an enum-specifier of D ().

discussion.p:
The second bullet looks strange to a language lawyer: an enum type can only be declared by opaque-enum-declarations and enum-specifiers (), and only the latter are definitions ( p2). That is, D must be an enum-specifier. What does it mean to say "an enum-specifier of an enum-specifier"?
I think, instead of saying "an enum-specifier of D", the second bullet could simply refer to D as an enum-specifier.

blockquote.p: -30- A type T is enumerable from a point P if either

li.p: (30.1) — T is a class type complete at point P or
(30.2) — T is an enumeration type defined by a declaration an enum-specifier D () such that D is reachable from P but P does not occur within an enum-specifier of D ().
